code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # simple verb mapping for readability
    # Mapping each predicate to a verb phrase and optional unit for numeric values
    predicate_info = {
        "cityServed": ("serves", None),
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": ("has an elevation of", "metres"),
        "location": ("is located at", None),
        "operatingOrganisation": ("is operated by", None),
        "runwayLength": ("has a runway length of", "metres"),
        "runwayName": ("has runway named", None),
        "country": ("is in", None),
        "isPartOf": ("is part of", None),
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": ("has a first runway length of", "feet"),
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": ("has a first runway surface of", None),
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": ("has a third runway length of", "feet"),
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": ("has ICAO code", None),
        "locationIdentifier": ("has location identifier", None),
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": ("has elevation of", "feet"),
        "iataLocationIdentifier": ("has IATA code", None),
        "nativeName": ("has native name", None),
        "leaderParty": ("belongs to party", None),
        "capital": ("has capital", None),
        "language": ("speaks", None),
        "leader": ("is led by", None),
        "owner": ("is owned by", None),
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": ("has a first runway length of", "metres"),
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": ("has a fourth runway surface of", None),
        "5thRunwayNumber": ("has fifth runway number", None),
        "largestCity": ("has largest city", None),
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": ("has a fourth runway length of", "feet"),
        "1stRunwayNumber": ("has first runway number", None),
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": ("has elevation of", "metres"),
        "administrativeArrondissement": ("is in administrative arrondissement", None),
        "mayor": ("has mayor", None),
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": ("has second runway surface of", None),
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": ("has third runway surface of", None),
        "runwaySurfaceType": ("has runway surface of", None),
        "officialLanguage": ("has official language", None),
        "city": ("is city of", None),
        "jurisdiction": ("has jurisdiction over", None),
        "demonym": ("has demonym", None),
        "aircraftHelicopter": ("operates helicopter", None),
        "transportAircraft": ("operates transport aircraft", None),
        "currency": ("uses currency", None),
        "headquarter": ("has headquarter at", None),
        "class": ("belongs to class", None),
        "division": ("belongs to division", None),
        "order": ("belongs to order", None),
        "regionServed": ("serves region", None),
        "leaderTitle": ("has leader title", None),
        "hubAirport": ("has hub airport", None),
        "aircraftFighter": ("operates fighter aircraft", None),
        "attackAircraft": ("operates attack aircraft", None),
        "battle": ("participated in battle", None),
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": ("has fifth runway surface of", None),
        "countySeat": ("has county seat", None),
        "chief": ("has chief", None),
        "foundedBy": ("was founded by", None),
        "postalCode": ("has postal code", None),
        "areaCode": ("has area code", None),
        "foundingYear": ("was founded in", None),
        "ceremonialCounty": ("is in ceremonial county", None),
    }
    # Group predicates by subject to build a single complex sentence per subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        verb, unit = predicate_info.get(t.predicate, ("has", None))
        obj = t.object
        # Strip surrounding quotes if present
        if isinstance(obj, str) and obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        # Append unit for numeric objects when a unit is defined
        if unit:
            try:
                num = float(obj)
                # Use integer representation when possible
                num_str = str(int(num)) if num.is_integer() else str(num)
                obj = f"{num_str} {unit}"
            except ValueError:
                pass  # keep original obj if not a number
        subject_map[t.subject].append(f"{verb} {obj}")

    parts = []
    for subj, actions in subject_map.items():
        if len(actions) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subj} {actions[0]}")
        else:
            # Join multiple actions with commas and an 'and' before the last one
            if len(actions) == 2:
                actions_str = " and ".join(actions)
            else:
                actions_str = ", ".join(actions[:-1]) + ", and " + actions[-1]
            parts.append(f"{subj} {actions_str}")

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single complex sentence
    if not parts:
        return ""
    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0]
    else:
        # Join multiple subject clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last one
        sentence = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
    # Capitalize first character of the sentence
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] if sentence else sentence
    # Ensure the sentence ends with a period
    return sentence.rstrip() + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
